message.content:
Thoroughly explore the mobile app at C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app. I need to understand:
1. Full directory structure (src/, assets/, etc.)
2. Current theme system (src/theme/colors.ts and any other theme files)
3. App.tsx and navigation setup (RootNavigator.tsx, types.ts)
4. Current Screen.tsx and PrimaryButton.tsx components
5. Current auth screens (LoginScreen.tsx, SignUpScreen.tsx)
6. Current HomeFeedScreen.tsx
7. Current PostDetailScreen.tsx
8. Current ProfileScreen.tsx
9. Current API layer (mobileApi.ts, socialApi.ts)
10. Current lexical.ts content handling
11. package.json for existing dependencies
12. react-native.config.js if it exists
13. Any existing assets/fonts directory

Read all of these files fully. This is critical for understanding what exists before we start modifying.

content.text: I'll systematically explore the mobile app. Let me start with the directory structure and then read all the key files in parallel.

input.description: List all files in the mobile app excluding node_modules, .git, android, ios

1→import React from 'react';
     2→import {StyleSheet, Text, View} from 'react-native';
     3→import {useQuery} from '@tanstack/react-query';
     4→import {FeatureTodoCard} from '../../components/FeatureTodoCard';
     5→import {Screen} from '../../components/Screen';
     6→import {mobileApi} from '../../lib/api/mobileApi';
     7→import {colors} from '../../theme/colors';

     9→export function UniverseScreen() {
    10→  const universeQuery = useQuery({
    11→    queryKey: ['universe-posts-default'],
    12→    queryFn: () =>
    13→      mobileApi.getUniversePosts(
    14→        {
    15→          minX: -5000,
    16→          maxX: 5000,
    17→          minY: -5000,
    18→          maxY: 5000,
    19→        },
    20→        120,
    21→      ),
    22→  });
    23→
    24→  return (
    25→    <Screen>
    26→      <Text style={styles.title}>Universe</Text>
    27→      <FeatureTodoCard
    28→        title="Universe shell ready"
    29→        description="Native viewport and constellation rendering hooks are reserved here. Current work focused on app foundation, API parity, and core product flows."
    30→      />
    31→      <View style={styles.card}>
    32→        <Text style={styles.cardTitle}>Viewport snapshot</Text>
    33→        <Text style={styles.cardText}>
    34→          {universeQuery.isLoading
    35→            ? 'Loading posts...'
    36→            : `Loaded stars/posts: ${universeQuery.data?.data?.length ?? 0}`}
    37→        </Text>
    38→      </View>
    39→    </Screen>
    40→  );
    41→}
    42→

    56→  },

    59→    fontWeight: '700',
    60→    fontSize: 15,
    61→  },
    62→  cardText: {
    63→    color: colors.textMuted,
    64→    fontSize: 14,
    65→  },

content.text:
I now have a complete picture of the entire mobile app. Here is the full report.

---

## Complete Exploration of `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app`

### 1. Full Directory Structure

The project is a monorepo using npm workspaces. All app code lives under `apps/mobile/`.

```
iskrib_mobile_app/
  package.json                          # Root workspace config
  package-lock.json
  README.md
  apps/
    mobile/
      App.tsx                           # Root component
      index.js                          # Entry point (AppRegistry)
      app.json                          # {name: "mobile", displayName: "mobile"}
      package.json                      # Dependencies
      tsconfig.json                     # Extends @react-native/typescript-config
      babel.config.js                   # RN preset + dotenv + reanimated plugins
      metro.config.js                   # Monorepo-aware Metro config
      .env / .env.example               # Environment variables
      env.d.ts                          # @env module declarations
      Gemfile                           # Ruby (iOS CocoaPods)
      jest.config.js / jest.setup.js    # Test config
      .eslintrc.js / .prettierrc.js     # Linting
      __tests__/
        App.test.tsx
        apiClient.test.ts
      src/
        config/
          env.ts                        # Environment config builder
        theme/
          colors.ts                     # Color palette (ONLY theme file)
        navigation/
          RootNavigator.tsx             # All navigation setup
          types.ts                      # Param list types
        features/
          auth/
            AuthProvider.tsx            # Supabase auth context + hooks
        lib/
          supabase.ts                   # Supabase AuthClient creation
          queryClient.ts                # React Query client config
          api/
            apiClient.ts               # Generic fetch wrapper with retry/timeout
            mobileApi.ts               # Journals, users, search, canvas, freedom wall, universe
            socialApi.ts                # Likes, comments, bookmarks, notifications, reposts
            storyApi.ts                 # Full CRUD for stories, chapters, voting, library
          content/
            lexical.ts                  # Lexical JSON parsing/creation utilities
        components/
          Screen.tsx                    # SafeAreaView + ScrollView wrapper
          PrimaryButton.tsx             # Pressable button (primary/secondary/danger)
          AppErrorBoundary.tsx          # Error boundary with retry
          FeatureTodoCard.tsx           # Placeholder card for unbuilt features
        screens/
          Auth/
            LoginScreen.tsx
            SignUpScreen.tsx
          Home/
            HomeFeedScreen.tsx          # Infinite feed + Write button
            ExploreScreen.tsx           # Search users/journals
            StoriesScreen.tsx           # Placeholder tab (unused - StoryBrowserScreen is used instead)
            PostDetailScreen.tsx        # Full post view + like/bookmark/comment/repost
          Editor/
            JournalEditorScreen.tsx     # Rich text editor (pell-rich-editor)
          Profile/
            ProfileScreen.tsx           # Basic profile + sign out
          Notifications/
            NotificationsScreen.tsx     # List with mark-read/delete
          Stories/
            StoryBrowserScreen.tsx      # Browse all stories with status filters
            StoryDetailScreen.tsx       # Story detail with vote/library/chapter list
            StoryEditorScreen.tsx       # Create/edit story metadata + tags
            StoryDashboardScreen.tsx    # "My Stories" management
            StoryLibraryScreen.tsx      # User's saved stories
            StoryChapterManagerScreen.tsx  # Create/publish/delete chapters
            StoryChapterEditorScreen.tsx   # Rich text chapter editor
            StoryChapterReaderScreen.tsx   # Reading view + scroll progress + comments
          Advanced/
            CanvasScreen.tsx            # Placeholder with gallery API wired
            FreedomWallScreen.tsx       # Placeholder with week/items API wired
            UniverseScreen.tsx          # Placeholder with viewport API wired
```

### 2. Theme System

There is exactly **one theme file**: `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\theme\colors.ts`

```ts
export const colors = {
  background: '#0b1220',     // Deep dark navy
  surface: '#111a2b',        // Card/panel backgrounds
  surfaceAlt: '#17233b',     // Secondary surface (toolbars, inputs)
  text: '#edf2ff',           // Primary text (near-white)
  textMuted: '#9aa8c7',      // Secondary/muted text
  accent: '#40c5a4',         // Green/teal accent (buttons, active states)
  danger: '#ff6b6b',         // Red for errors/destructive actions
  border: '#26334f',         // Border color for cards/inputs
  white: '#ffffff',          // Pure white (button labels)
};
```

There are NO other theme files -- no spacing tokens, no typography tokens, no dark/light mode toggle. All components directly import `colors` from this single file. The navigation theme in `RootNavigator.tsx` extends React Navigation's `DarkTheme` with these color values.

### 3. App.tsx and Navigation Setup

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\App.tsx`** (77 lines):
- Wraps everything in `GestureHandlerRootView` > `SafeAreaProvider` > `AppErrorBoundary` > `QueryClientProvider` > `AuthProvider`
- Sets `StatusBar` to light-content with `colors.background`
- Has a fallback UI if `AuthProvider` fails to load
- Renders `<RootNavigator />`

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\navigation\RootNavigator.tsx`** (199 lines):
- Three navigator layers:
  1. **AuthStack** (NativeStackNavigator): `Login`, `SignUp`
  2. **MainTabs** (BottomTabNavigator): `Home` (HomeFeedScreen), `Explore` (ExploreScreen), `Stories` (StoryBrowserScreen), `Notifications` (NotificationsScreen), `Profile` (ProfileScreen)
  3. **RootStack** (NativeStackNavigator): wraps Auth OR (Main + all detail screens)
- Conditional rendering: `!session` shows AuthStack, otherwise MainTabs + stack screens
- Detail screens on RootStack: `PostDetail`, `JournalEditor`, `StoryDetail`, `StoryEditor`, `StoryDashboard`, `StoryLibrary`, `StoryChapterManager`, `StoryChapterEditor`, `StoryChapterReader`, `Canvas`, `FreedomWall`, `Universe`
- Custom `navTheme` extends `DarkTheme` with app colors

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\navigation\types.ts`** (29 lines):
```ts
export type AuthStackParamList = {
  Login: undefined;
  SignUp: undefined;
};

export type MainTabParamList = {
  Home: undefined;
  Explore: undefined;
  Stories: undefined;
  Notifications: undefined;
  Profile: undefined;
};

export type RootStackParamList = {
  Auth: undefined;
  Main: undefined;
  PostDetail: { journalId: string; slug?: string };
  JournalEditor: { mode: 'create' | 'edit'; journalId?: string };
  StoryDetail: {storyId: string};
  StoryEditor: {storyId?: string};
  StoryDashboard: undefined;
  StoryLibrary: undefined;
  StoryChapterManager: {storyId: string};
  StoryChapterEditor: {storyId: string; chapterId: string};
  StoryChapterReader: {storyId: string; chapterId: string};
  Canvas: undefined;
  FreedomWall: undefined;
  Universe: undefined;
};
```

### 4. Screen.tsx and PrimaryButton.tsx

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\components\Screen.tsx`** (31 lines):
- Props: `children`, `scroll` (default true), `contentContainerStyle`
- Wraps content in `SafeAreaView` with `colors.background`
- If `scroll=true`, uses `ScrollView`; otherwise uses `View`
- Default content padding: 16px, gap: 12px

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\components\PrimaryButton.tsx`** (61 lines):
- Props: `label`, `onPress`, `loading?`, `disabled?`, `kind?` (`'primary' | 'secondary' | 'danger'`)
- Uses `Pressable` with press opacity effect
- Primary: `colors.accent` background
- Secondary: `colors.surfaceAlt` background with border
- Danger: `colors.danger` background
- Shows `ActivityIndicator` when loading
- Border radius: 10, padding: 12v/16h

### 5. Auth Screens

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\screens\Auth\LoginScreen.tsx`** (103 lines):
- Email + password `TextInput` fields
- Validation: email >4 chars, password >5 chars to enable submit
- Calls `useAuth().signIn()` with try/catch + Alert on error
- "Create Account" secondary button navigates to SignUp
- Title: "Iskrib Mobile"

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\screens\Auth\SignUpScreen.tsx`** (108 lines):
- Same layout as Login but password requires 8+ chars
- Calls `useAuth().signUp()`, then shows Alert "Check your inbox" and navigates back to Login
- "Back to Login" secondary button

### 6. HomeFeedScreen.tsx

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\screens\Home\HomeFeedScreen.tsx`** (255 lines):
- Uses `useInfiniteQuery` with cursor-based pagination (PAGE_SIZE = 10)
- Header row with "Home Feed" title + "Write" button (navigates to JournalEditor)
- Quick action row: Canvas, Freedom Wall, Universe buttons
- `FlatList` rendering with pull-to-refresh and infinite scroll
- Each post card shows: title, author name, post_type, preview text (first 180 chars extracted from Lexical JSON), stats row (likes, comments, bookmarks)
- `extractPlainText()` inline helper parses Lexical JSON content
- `resolveCount()` and `normalizeCount()` helpers handle aggregate vs flat count fields
- Pressing a card navigates to `PostDetail`

### 7. PostDetailScreen.tsx

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\screens\Home\PostDetailScreen.tsx`** (437 lines):
- Fetches single journal via `mobileApi.getJournalById()` and comments via `socialApi.getComments()`
- Body text extracted via `extractPlainTextFromLexical()` from `lexical.ts`
- Four mutations: `likeMutation`, `bookmarkMutation`, `addCommentMutation`, `repostMutation`
- Like/Bookmark rendered as pill-style pressable buttons with active states (green border + dark green bg)
- Comments section with author/body/timestamp, plus input field to add comment
- Repost section with optional caption and "Repost to feed" button
- All mutations invalidate relevant query keys on success

### 8. ProfileScreen.tsx

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\screens\Profile\ProfileScreen.tsx`** (90 lines):
- Very basic: shows profile title, user card (name, username, email), and a "Sign Out" danger button
- Fetches user data via `mobileApi.getUserData(user.id)`
- No avatar display, no bio, no post list, no edit functionality

### 9. API Layer

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\lib\api\apiClient.ts`** (244 lines):
- Core `apiRequest<T>()` function wrapping `fetch()`
- Custom error classes: `ApiError`, `ApiTimeoutError`, `ApiNetworkError`
- Default timeout: 15s, default retries: 2 (GET/HEAD/OPTIONS only)
- Exponential backoff with jitter, respects `Retry-After` header
- URL building: prepends `MOBILE_ENV.API_BASE_URL` to relative paths
- Auto-sets `Accept: application/json` header
- Handles abort controller, timeout, and caller-provided signal

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\lib\api\mobileApi.ts`** (301 lines):
- Types exported: `UserPreview`, `JournalItem`, `CursorPage<T>`, `NotificationItem`
- Methods: `getJournals`, `getJournalById`, `searchUsers`, `searchJournals`, `getNotifications`, `getUserData`, `getCanvasGallery`, `getFreedomWallWeeks`, `getCurrentFreedomWallWeek`, `getFreedomWallItems`, `getUniversePosts`, `saveJournal`
- `saveJournal` constructs a Lexical JSON payload from plain text and sends via FormData

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\lib\api\socialApi.ts`** (195 lines):
- Types: `JournalComment`
- Methods: `likeJournal`, `addComment`, `getComments`, `toggleBookmark`, `getBookmarks`, `getNotificationsCount`, `markNotificationRead`, `deleteNotification`, `getUnreadNotifications`, `createRepost`
- Note: bookmark endpoint is `/addBoorkmark` (typo matches server)

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\lib\api\storyApi.ts`** (436 lines):
- Types: `StoryItem`, `StoryChapterSummary`, `StoryChapter`, `StoryListResponse`, `StoryStatus`, `StoryPrivacy`
- Full CRUD methods: `getStories`, `getStoryById`, `getMyStories`, `getMyLibrary`, `getUserStories`, `createStory`, `updateStory`, `deleteStory`
- Chapter methods: `createChapter`, `getChapter`, `updateChapter`, `deleteChapter`, `reorderChapters`
- Social methods: `toggleVote`, `toggleLibrary`, `getChapterComments`, `getChapterCommentCounts`, `addChapterComment`
- Reading progress: `saveReadingProgress`, `getReadingProgress`
- Image uploads use FormData with `{uri, type, name}` pattern

### 10. Lexical.ts Content Handling

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\lib\content\lexical.ts`** (83 lines):
- `LexicalNode` type: `{type?, text?, children?}`
- `stripHtml(html)`: Removes HTML tags and ` `, collapses whitespace
- `toLexicalFromPlainText(plainText)`: Creates a minimal Lexical JSON document with a single paragraph + text node
- `collectText(node, bag)`: Recursive tree walker that collects all text node values
- `extractPlainTextFromLexical(rawContent)`: Parses Lexical JSON (string or object), recursively extracts all text, joins with spaces. Falls back to raw string on parse failure.

### 11. package.json Dependencies

**Root** (`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\package.json`):
- Workspace: `apps/*`, `packages/*`
- Scripts delegate to `@iskrib/mobile`

**App** (`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\package.json`):
- **React/RN**: react 19.2.3, react-native 0.84.0
- **Navigation**: @react-navigation/native 7.1.28, /bottom-tabs 7.14.0, /native-stack 7.13.0
- **State/Data**: @tanstack/react-query 5.90.21, @supabase/supabase-js 2.97.0
- **Storage**: @react-native-async-storage/async-storage 3.0.1
- **UI/Animation**: react-native-reanimated 4.2.2, react-native-gesture-handler 2.30.0, react-native-safe-area-context 5.7.0, react-native-screens 4.24.0
- **Editor**: react-native-pell-rich-editor 1.10.0
- **Graphics**: @shopify/react-native-skia 2.4.21
- **WebView**: react-native-webview 13.16.0
- **Worklets**: react-native-worklets 0.7.4
- **Validation**: zod 4.3.6
- **Dev**: typescript 5.8.3, jest 29.6.3, eslint 8.x, prettier 2.8.8, react-native-dotenv 3.4.11
- **Engine**: node >= 22.11.0

### 12. react-native.config.js

**Does NOT exist** in the project. Only found inside `node_modules/` for dependencies (safe-area-context, webview, screens, react-native itself). There is no custom project-level `react-native.config.js`.

### 13. Assets/Fonts Directory

**No `assets/` or `fonts/` directories exist** anywhere under `apps/mobile/src/`. There are no custom fonts configured. The `.tmp-assets/` directory under `apps/mobile/` contains only auto-generated drawable PNGs from node_modules (react-native LogBox, pell-rich-editor toolbar icons, react-navigation element icons) -- these are build artifacts, not custom assets.

### Additional Files of Note

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\features\auth\AuthProvider.tsx`** (172 lines):
- Creates React context with `user`, `session`, `loading`, `authError`, `signIn`, `signUp`, `signOut`
- Uses Supabase AuthClient directly (not full `createClient`) -- imported from `lib/supabase.ts`
- Session persistence via AsyncStorage
- Listens to `onAuthStateChange` for session updates
- Guards against misconfigured Supabase with descriptive error messages

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\config\env.ts`** (142 lines):
- Reads from `@env` module (react-native-dotenv)
- Three env vars: `ISK_MOBILE_API_BASE_URL`, `ISK_MOBILE_SUPABASE_URL`, `ISK_MOBILE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`
- Default API URL: `http://10.0.2.2:3000/api` (Android emulator localhost)
- Validates URLs, detects placeholder values, produces warnings
- Exports `MOBILE_ENV`, `IS_SUPABASE_CONFIGURED`, `ENV_WARNINGS`

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\lib\supabase.ts`** (42 lines):
- Creates a minimal Supabase client using only `AuthClient` from `@supabase/auth-js`
- Uses AsyncStorage for session persistence
- Extracts project ref from URL for storage key naming
- Returns `null` if Supabase is not configured

**`C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\lib\queryClient.ts`** (44 lines):
- staleTime: 30s, gcTime: 5min
- Custom retry logic using `ApiError`/`ApiTimeoutError`/`ApiNetworkError`
- Max 2 retries for queries, 0 for mutations
- Exponential retry delay capped at 4s
